texts.full_text: visualizing predictive uncertainty in numerical ensembles through Bayesian model averaging. Numerical ensemble forecasting is a powerful tool that drives many risk analysis efforts and decision making tasks. These ensembles are composed of individual simulations that each uniquely model a possible outcome for a common event of interest: e.g., the direction and force of a hurricane, or the path of travel and mortality rate of a pandemic. This paper presents a new visual strategy to help quantify and characterize a numerical ensemble's predictive uncertainty: i.e., the ability for ensemble constituents to accurately and consistently predict an event of interest based on ground truth observations. Our strategy employs a Bayesian framework to first construct a statistical aggregate from t...he individual simulations, effectively creating a weighted average of each model's predictions based on their relative performance and uncertainty. Neutrophils from periodontally healthy individuals were treated with nicotine, cotinine and CSE before stimulation with Fusobacterium nucleatum, IgG-opsonized Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli lipopolysaccharide. Total and extracellular reactive oxygen species (ROS) generation was determined by luminol/isoluminol chemiluminescence. Activation of NFκB in oral epithelial cells was determined by immunocytochemistry. Smoke extract alone caused increased neutTo determine the effect of nicotine, cotinine and cigarette smoke extract (CSE) on the neutrophil respiratory burst and their effect on activation of the nuclear factor-κB (NFκB) pathway in oral epithelium, the following experimental approach was employed. Neutrophils from periodontally healthy individuals were treated with nicotine, cotinine, and CSE before stimulation with Fusobacterium nucleatum, IgG-opsonized Staphylococcus aureus, and Escherichia coli lipopolysaccharide. Total and extracellular reactive oxygen species (ROS) generation was determined by luminol/isoluminol chemiluminescence. Activation of NFκB in oral epithelial cells was determined by immunocytochemistry. Smoke extract alone caused increased neutrophil activation, as evidenced by the elevated levels of ROS production. This suggests that the components of cigarette smoke, including nicotine and cotinine, directly stimulate neutrophils, potentially leading to a heightened immune response. However, this increased activation could also contribute to tissue damage and inflammation in the oral cavity, as well as exacerbate periodontal disease progression. Furthermore, the activation of the NFκB pathway in oral epithelial cells demonstrated that exposure to cigarette smoke extracts led to a significant upregulation of this pro-inflammatory pathway. This implies that smoking may not only affect the immune cells but also modulate the behavior of oral epithelial cells, potentially leading to changes in gene expression and cellular functions that contribute to the pathogenesis of oral diseases. In conclusion, the study findings indicate that nicotine, cotinine, and cigarette smoke extract have a significant impact on neutrophil respiratory burst activity and the activation of the NFκB pathway in oral epithelium. texts.full_text: ic hepatitis B (CHB) affects 240 million people worldwide^[@try117C1]^ and kills around 700 000 people annually through the complications of hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) and cirrhosis, predominantly in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs).^[@try117C2],[@try117C3]^ After a long period of neglect,^[@try117C4]^ hepatitis B has become a focus of global health policy. Following the World Health Assembly resolution on viral hepatitis in 2014, the World Health Organization (WHO) published guidelines for CHB in March 2015^[@try117C5]^ and the Global Health Sector Strategy on Viral Hepatitis in June 2016.^[@try117C6]^ These documents describe a goal to eliminate viral hepatitis as a public health threat by 2030, including a 65% reduction in mortality from viral hepatitis, from a starting point in 2016 where \<1% of individuals with viral hepatitis currently receive treatment. In the absence of large-scale international funding, the onus is on member countries to establish national guidelines and provide treatment. Therapy with nucleoside reverse-transcriptase inhibitors such as tenofovir controls viral replication,^[@try117C7]^ reverses fibrosis^[@try117C8]^ and reduces the risk of HCC.^[@try117C9]^ These drugs are safe and well tolerated in CHB.^[@try117C10]^ One major barrier to dru===================== Introducti

texts.full_text: t can respond appropriately), while those infants with the non-risk version of the gene did. At these early ages, the researchers found, it didn't appear to matter whether mothers were sensitive or not. However, by the time the infants were 12 months old, the pattern changed. Infants with the risk form of the gene who also had mothers who were highly sensitive now showed the expected cardiac response while they were exposed to the stressful situation. Those infants with the risk form of the gene who had insensitive mothers continued to show the ineffective cardiac response to the stressor. These findings suggest that although genes play a role in the development of physiological responses to stress, environmental experience (such as mothers' sensitive care-giving behavior) can have a strong influence, enough to change the effect that genes have on physiology very early in life. The researchers suggest this may be because of the cumulative effect on infants of exposure to their mothers' behavior. "Our findings provide further support for the notion that the development of complex behavioral and physiological responses is not the result of nature or nurture, but rather a combination of the two," said Cathi Propper, research scientist at the University of North Carolina-Chapel Hill and the study's lead author. "They also illustrate the importance of parenting not just for the development of children's behavior, but for the underlying physiological mechanisms that support this behavior." Ginger Moore, a study co-author and assistant professor of psychology at Penn State, added, "Lastly, infancy is an important time for developing behavioral and biological processes. Although these processes will continue to change over time, parenting can have important positive effects even when children have inherited a genetic vulnerability to problematic behaviors." With that depletion advantage, XTO is able to devote a good chunk of its annual capital expenditures, slated at $2.6 billion this year, to expanding output, not simply maintaining it. XTO was able to assemble its portfolio of energy-producing properties over the past 20 years because major oil companies like ExxonMobil, Chevron and Total decided to shed them and focus their exploration efforts on the search for elephant-sized fields overseas. XTO then unleashed its top-flight team of geologists and engineers to dramatically add to existing reserves. "We put an engineer with 20 years of experience on something where the majors might have put a trainee," Simpson says. "The majors have to put their A teams on their A-size assets." XTO is very bullish on one of its largest deals ever, the $2.5 billion purchase in July of a group of gas-producing fields from Dominion Resources, the Virginia electric utility. Those fields will complement existing XTO properties in the Piceance Basin of Colorado and San Juan Basin of New Mexico, while giving XTO new exposure to fields in Wyoming and Utah. An upbeat Simpson believes these Dominion properties could hold 50% to 100% more than the current level of proved reserves of 1 trillion cubic feet of gas. XTO's existing reserves primarily are located in East Texas, in the Barnett Shale region around Fort Worth and in New Mexico. XTO, like other independents, is looking to put some of its long-lived assets into a master limited partnership. The reason: partnerships command higher valuations than independent energy outfits, thanks to high dividends, and there are tax advantages. This move could boost the stock by highlighting the appeal of XTO's assets. texts.full_text: Shopping Health Tools mole A benign growth on the skin (usually tan, brown, or fblack) called an adenoma. It is caused by a rapid increase in melanocytes and may be hereditary. There are several types of moles: 1. Junctional Nevi: These occur when pigment cells cluster at the top layer (epidermis). They tend to have smooth edges, flat tops or slightly raised surfaces. 2. Compound Nevis : In this type melanocytes extend down into both layers - epithelium and derma 3. Intradermal nevus: These are formed in deeper skin tissues when the cells of pigment grow abnormally. The vast majority (99%) moles appear during childhood or early adulthood, but they can also occur later on after 30 years old. Moles that have existed for many decades may darken and become raised. They might be itchy at times too. Most people get between ten to forty of these skin lesions by the age of thirty. In some cases moles could develop into melanoma - a deadly form cancer if not detected early on, although this is quite rare (about 1 in every million). So getting them checked regularly and taking care with self-examinations can be important. If you notice any changes to your mole such as size or shape change; color variation from the usual brown/black tone of it ; surface that bleeds, then immediate medical advice is recommended. A dermatologist may decide on a biopsy (sampling) for diagnosis if there are concerns about its nature. This can be done in their rooms and only takes minutes with local anaesthetic. texts.full_text: after two series on Comedy Central there would be no more new Alternative Comedy Experienceshows forthcoming due to a lack of interest from advertisers and viewers. This news comes at an interesting time for the comedy circuit in Britain. While mainstream stand-up continues to thrive with big names like Russell Howard drawing large crowds, it’s becoming increasingly difficult for alternative comedians—those who don’t conform to traditional comedic styles or themes—to find platforms where they can showcase their work without having to compromise. Howard has become a poster boy of sorts for the new wave of British comedy. With his energetic and often politically charged performances, he's managed to bridge the gap between alternative humor and mainstream appeal. His success is evident in both his packed-out tours and high ratings on television shows like "The Russell Howard Hour." In contrast, Stewart Lee has long been a champion of what might be called 'anti-comedy'—a style that often challenges audience expectations with its deconstructionist approach to stand-up. While this makes for intellectually stimulating performances, it can also limit the size and diversity of his audiences. Lee’s comments about Comedy Central's decision highlight an ongoing tension in British comedy: while there is a clear appetite among certain segments of the population for more experimental forms of humor, these often struggle to find consistent support from broadcasters. This situation leaves many alternative comedians with fewer opportunities to reach wider audiences and sustain their careers. It remains to be seen whether new platforms or innovative Approaches will emerge to fill this gap in the comedy landscape. texts.full_text: to become true. Analyze both of these statements for a couple of seconds. You are stating like these are factual future events. But do you really know for certain what the outcome is going to be? The answer, most often than not, is no. The way we frame our thoughts and expectations has an immense impact on how situations unfold in reality. Your subconscious mind listens carefully to everything you tell it; hence if you repeatedly affirm something as difficult or unpleasant before even giving yourself a chance, your actions will likely align with these negative predictions. To set yourself up for success: 1. Change the Narrative: Start by replacing pessimistic thoughts and statements with ones. - Instead of “This is going to be very difficult,” say, “I can handle this challenge.” - Replace “I’m not going to like this” with “I will find something in this experience.” 2. Visualize Success: Spend a few moments each day visualizing yourself succeeding at the task or goal you are facing. See it happening as if it’s already true. 3. Take Small Steps: Break down your goals into manageable steps and focus on making progress one step at a time. This helps to build momentum and confidence. 4. This has led, as an example, to applications where cameras record everything but the data is saved locally and can be recalled then later in the office. This might be a good thing for the public interest when evaluating actions and procedures, but no use what comes to operational support. Another example is the paperwork on the road. If there is not always connection. the documents need to be downloaded later and there is no possibility to check the background information often needed to fill in all the documents. This often leads to reluctance to actually perform the tasks in the field and they are postponed to be done in the office. Both of these examples, as being improvements for sure, don’t bring the change in efficiency expected by the investors. The key to significant efficiency improvement is the always online connectivity. If the users can be certain that the connectivity is there, the whole operational picture changes. Some of the users claim that the always online connectivity has brought a change that has been “bigger than changing from horses to cars”! How can this be achieved then? The first example is the video streaming. With online video streaming the whole concept becomes a command and control solution. Wherever anything worth sharing happens, the unit in the field can share the view with the headquarters and with any other task relevant units. Data is shared online and in this case a picture is truly worth thousand words, minimizing also unnecessary voice traffic. Also recording is possible in this scenario, but the application and use is totally different and recording can happen in vehicle or command and control center. texts.full_text: e not observed during surgery. Histopathological examination revealed a well-circumscribed mass composed of spindle cells with eosinophilic cytoplasm, arranged in fascicles and bundles typical for Immunohistochemistry was positive for smooth muscle actin (SMA) and desmin but negative for S-100 protein, chromogranin A, synaptophysin, CD34, c-kit (CD117), DOG1, and Pan CK. These findings confirmed the diagnosis of a leiomyoma. The patient had an uneventful postoperative course and was discharged on the 8th day after surgery with no complications reported at follow-up appointments over two years later. To our knowledge, this is the first report in literature describing primary leiomyoma as a distinct clinicopathological entity outside of case reports involving patients where metastasis or co-occurrence with lesions could not be ruled out. Primary leiomyomas are benign tumors that most commonly originate from uterine smooth muscle but can occur at various extragenital sites. In the pancreas, primary leiomyoma is exceedingly rare and presents a diagnostic challenge due to its non-specific clinical presentation and imaging characteristics similar to those of other pancreatic neoplasms. In conclusion, although extremely uncommon, this case highlights that primary pancreatic leiomyomas should be included in the diagnosis for encapsulated, well-circumscribed tumors of the pancreas with benign radiological features. texts.full_text: family Alligatoridae, but has since been reclassified as a globidontan. It is the oldest and most primitive globidontan known to date, with fossils found in early Cretaceous deposits in Europe and North America, dating back around 125 million years. The name Acynodon means "pointless tooth" due to its distinctive dental morphology, which features broad, blunt teeth that are unlike those of modern alligatorids. This unique characteristic suggests a diet different from that of other crocodilians, possibly more focused on crushing and grinding tough plant material or hard-shelled prey rather than the typical meat-based diet of most crocodilian species. texts.full_text: asset that is sometimes out there are stock options. For many parties who work in a corporate environment, they may be awarded stock options as part of their employment. Stock options can be vested or non-vested. The difference between whether a stock option is vested or non-vested is really the second step after realizing they exist. If they are non-vested, they are generally do not constitute a property interest subject to division in a divorce. However, if they are vested stock options, then the question is whether they were vested during the marriage. If they were vested during the marriage, they are generally considered marital property subject to division in divorce. But in trying todivide them equally, judges also examine several considerations: 1. When and how the options became vested: Was it before or after the couple divorced? 2. The husband's contributions (if any) towards buying those stock options: Did they work for that firm during their marriage? If so, this would imply a marital effort in gaining these funds. 3. Whether bankruptcy was contemplated when awards were made: This would impact if granting the non-owner spouse an interest is equitable. Valuing vested but not already exercised stock options can often be complex and often requires expert testimony or testimony from financial experts unfamiliar with different institutional systems. In bankruptcy proceedings, these stock options would have to remain unchanged until they're ready for exercise. Biography Tracy Dockray spent her early years growing up on the plains of Texas where she enjoyed reading and a large menagerie of pets. She moved to New York where she attained an MFA from Pratt. She moved from mural painting to puppet design to fabric design before she found her passion creating children's books. She currently resides in Greenwich Village, New York with her husband, children, and pets. She is mostly known for the popular series by Beverly Cleary that she illustrated which includes the Ramona Series and the Mouse and the Motorcycle Series. Most recently she illustrated the Fix-It Friends Series by Nicole C. Kear. Altogether, Tracy Dockray has illustrated over 25 books. Her careers throughout the years included the following: puppetry sculpture illustrating painting murals school playgrounds hospitals children's rooms Information Tracy Dockray has participated in the Learning Inc. Literacy Programs as well as the Learning Leaders Author's Read-aloud Program that is located in New York. She has contributed to the annual Children's Literary Event that is located at the Bethlehem Center in Dallas, Texas. Tracy Dockray is a member of the Society of Illustrators. texts.full_text: tate had to approve it before it could come into force. In most, this was done by a Parliamentary vote (referendums are banned in Germany) but in others, it was put to a referendum. Spain was the first country to hold a referendum on the Constitution. The referendum approved the Constitution by 76% of the votes, although participation was only around 43%. On 29 May 2005 the French public rejected the Constitution by margin of 55% to 45% on a turn out of 69%. And just three days later the Dutch rejected the constitution by a margin of 61% to 39% on a turnout of 62%. Notwithstanding the rejection in France and the Netherlands, Luxembourg held a referendum on 10 July 2005 approving the Constitution by 57% to 43%. It was the last referendum to be held on the Constitution as all of the other member states that had proposed to hold referendums cancelled them. Following the constitution's rejection by such central states, the EU leaders declared a "period of reflection" while they decided what to do next. This period ending with the Berlin Declaration on 25 March 2007 (which was the 50th anniversary of the Treaties of Rome). The declaration was intended to give a new impetus to finding a new institutional settlement. On 4 June 2007, this group, known as the Amato Group, presented its report. They proposed to establish a new Inter-Governmental Conference with a view to writing a new treaty which would rewrite the Treaty on European Union, amend the Treaty establishing the European Community and give the Charter of Fundamental Rights of the European Union a legally binding status. The new treaty would be based on the first and fourth parts of the Constitution, the rest of the Constitution's changes being achieved through amendments to the Treaty of Rome. We’ve seen how driven grouse shooting contributes to the illegal persecution of raptors (68% of those convicted of raptor persecution are gamekeepers), and that better regulation is needed. The intense management practices, including burning, also damages vital upland peat bogs in protected areas. As a result of this frustration, a number of petitions are also emerging. And while they don’t guarantee success, they do provide a window into public opinion that can’t be ignored. Last year, Mark Farrar launched his online plea to extend Scotland’s vicarious liability laws to England and Wales. This would hold landowners accountable for the crimeof the people they employ, and is a measure that’ s worked to protect wildlife in Scotland. The petition was a huge success and reached a total of 31,441 signatures, and the Government’ s response was a positive one. Chromatin assembly and remodeling are vicritical processes in cellular biology that involve the organization, compaction, and modification of chromosomal DNA to regulate gene expression and other nuclear functions. Chromatin is composed primarily of histone proteins around whichDNA wraps, forming nucleosomes as its basic unit. The dynamic nature of these structures allows for changes in accessibility of genetic material, enabling transcriptional regulation. Chromatin assembly refers to the process by which newly synthesized or modified DNA strands are packaged into chromatin fibers through interactions with core and linker histones. This involves ATP-dependent mechanisms facilitated by a variety of chaperone proteins that ensure proper folding around nucleosomes during replication as well as repair processes in eukaryotic cells. Chromatin remodeling, on the other hand, encompasses diverse enzymatic activities aimed at altering existing chromatin structures to modulate access and transcriptional potential. These modifications can include changes in histone post-translational modification states (such as acetylation or methylation), DNA bending through ATP-dependent motor proteins like SWI/SNF complexes, or replacement of one nucleosome core particle with another via the action of remodeling enzymes. Understanding these processes is crucial for elucidating mechanisms underlying development and disease. For instance, aberrant chromatin organization has been implicated in numerous pathologies including cancer where altered gene expression profiles can drive tumor progression. Therefore, studying how different factors influence assembly versus remodeling provides insights into potential therapeutic targets that could restore normal cellular function or halt pathological states associated with misregulated genome activity. Mikwid hamin, Based on the information provided, it seems that the description of the basket from the Atlantic Canada Visual Archives contains significant inaccuracies and misattributions. Here's a completed summary of the situation: While conducting a Google search, you discovered a misleading description of an indigenous basket on a website associated with the Atlantic Canada Visual Archives. The webpage attributed the creation of the basket to Sabattis Tomah in 1884 when he was just twelve years old and provided additional narrative details that seemed implausible. Upon reaching out to Micah Pawling, an expert on Sabattus Tomah, you learned that several aspects of the description raised concerns: 1. Age Inconsistency: At 12 years old in 1884, Sabattis Tomah was likely too young to create such a basket and certainly not known for his craftsmanship at that age. 2. Geographical Discrepancy: Both Sabattis Tomah and his father, Tomah Joseph, were from Motahkomikuk (Indian Township), not Sipayik (Pleasant Point) as stated in the description. 3.
